name: D+Q IPF randomized pilot primarily supports feasibility and tolerability boundaries
summary: The idiopathic pulmonary fibrosis randomized pilot abstract reports 12 participants randomized 1:1 to D+Q or placebo, complete scheduled dosing and assessment completion, more non-serious adverse events in the D+Q arm, no D+Q-related serious adverse events, and no meaningful between-arm differences in explored clinical measures.
statement: The randomized IPF pilot is useful for feasibility, tolerability, and endpoint-boundary evidence: all scheduled doses and planned assessments were completed, D+Q had more non-serious adverse events than placebo, and the trial was too small to support efficacy inference.
time_horizon: 3-week intervention period and planned pilot assessments
caveats: Treat as safety and feasibility evidence unless a later synthesis verifies a powered efficacy endpoint.
Functional outcomes should remain separate from aging-healthspan claims.
Article-table extraction is still needed for term-level adverse events and clinical-measure details beyond the abstract.

provenance.locator: PubMed PMID 36857968 abstract METHODS, FINDINGS, and INTERPRETATION
provenance.note: Abstract supports randomized N=12 sample size, D+Q dose schedule, full scheduled-dose and assessment completion, non-serious adverse-event imbalance, no D+Q-related serious adverse events, and underpowered clinical-measure interpretation.
